observation: Synchronized optical drop events have already passed a permutation sanity check.
81 cycles matched to electrochemistry; coarse cycle summaries do not fully explain events.
Synchronized event cycles are linked to unusually short frame-count regimes.
Detected 88 candidate particle/front regions; outputs are apparent front proxies, not calibrated diffusion coefficients.
Selected 10 cycle-86/116 candidate ROIs for next tracking from 16 scored candidates; selections are algorithmic and still need manual QC/spatial calibration.

Tracked 12 selected front ROIs at full-pixel crop scale; cycle 86 mean radius2 slope -0.127 px2/s, R2 0.178; cycle 116 mean radius2 slope -0.167 px2/s, R2 0.284. These are apparent front-motion proxies, not calibrated diffusion coefficients.
Evaluated 11 particle-ROI sequences with persistence, velocity, and low-rank DMD rollouts; DMD spectral radius 1.002.

Trained a PCA-ridge event-conditioned next-frame model on 11 selected ROI sequences; cycle 86 residual mean 0.00171; cycle 116 residual mean 0.000611. Persistence remains a strong recursive baseline, so residuals are the main degradation descriptor.
A leave-one-cycle residual CNN on 11 selected ROIs did not beat persistence; overall relative MSE improvement -4.23.

Combined rollout residual, front tracking, ROI physics, residual-CNN guardrails, and cycle evidence into 11 ROI joint modes; selected k=2 with silhouette 0.335. Top ROIs: cycle86_front4_obj9 score 2.86; cycle116_front3_obj9 score 1.72.

Compared 11 event ROIs against 16 matched control ROIs; strongest shifts: high_fraction_slope_per_frame p=0.000503; roi_mean_slope_per_frame p=0.00123. Leave-pair classifier did not generalize well, so controls are a guardrail.
Best transparent leave-one-particle event-forecast F1 is 0.435.
